summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host/system info to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package package.json declares a lifecycle script (line 35) that pulls in child_process, os, and https, gathers host identity (hostname, env vars, cwd) and POSTs the data to a hardcoded endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js corroborates this: it imports fs/https/child_process, calls os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), enumerates filesystem paths via fs.existsSync, and issues https.request/https.get plus curl invocations to the same https://ddjidd564.github.io destination (lines 142-161, 298, 323, 408). The destination is a third-party GitHub Pages site unrelated to any documented publisher and is being used as an exfiltration sink for installer system intelligence. The combination of hardcoded C2, system-info collection, and credential/environment scraping triggered on install constitutes an active supply-chain attack against any developer or build system that runs npm install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
